criteria.justification: Open Questions contains four unresolved product questions covering permissions, empty-state and thread scale, edit/delete behavior, and notifications rather than silently deciding them.
The UX Walkthrough explicitly labels empty, loading, success, and error states for the project comments flow.
The spec stays at product-behavior level and does not name libraries, route paths, schema fields, or other implementation mechanisms.
Explicitly Out of Scope lists several concrete exclusions such as realtime presence, attachments, and moderation or notification decisions.
Open Questions remains non-empty and captures unresolved destructive-action decisions around recoverability, confirmation, audit logging, and partial completion behavior.
Open Questions items 1 and 2 explicitly ask whether the delete is recoverable and whether the admin must confirm the bulk delete before it runs.
The UX Walkthrough explicitly addresses the no-eligible-reports empty state plus loading, success, and error outcomes for the bulk delete flow.
The spec never proposes a specific deletion mechanism or technology choice and stays focused on user-visible behavior and policy decisions.
Explicitly Out of Scope is present and non-trivial, excluding scheduled cleanup, broader lifecycle redesign, and unrelated archive or export work.
Open Questions is intentionally near-empty, stating that no further product clarification is needed for this small footer-label request.
The UX Walkthrough addresses empty, loading, success, and error relevance for the footer label, explicitly noting when a state is not meaningfully applicable.
The spec describes the footer behavior without naming code-level file access, libraries, or other implementation details.
